name: A logon was attempted using explicit credentials
applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 R2 and later; Windows 7 and later
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4648 records a process attempting to authenticate with explicitly supplied credentials, making it valuable for investigating credential use and lateral movement.
trigger_scenarios: Windows logs this event when a caller supplies a username and password to access a local or remote resource rather than using the current session token.

key_fields.explanation: The account and process that initiated the credential use. Compare it with the account whose credentials were supplied.
The target identity. Privileged, dormant, or service accounts used from unusual hosts deserve immediate review.
The destination resource and remote address. These fields identify the potential lateral-movement target.

false_positives: RunAs, scheduled tasks, backup systems, and deployment tools can use explicit credentials legitimately.
Administrative support staff may use alternate accounts to access servers or shares.
detection_notes: T1078 credential abuse is signaled when Account Whose Credentials Were Used is a privileged or service identity while Subject is a standard user, and Target Server Name is a new server. runas.exe, cmdkey.exe, or powershell.exe in Process Name followed by a Type 3 or Type 10 4624 is a concrete lateral-movement sequence.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4648
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, TargetAccount, ProcessName, IpAddress, TargetServerName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
spl_snippet:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4648
| table _time, host, SubjectUserName, TargetUserName, ProcessName, TargetServerName, NetworkAddress
sample_log:
Subject: CORP\jsmith
Account Whose Credentials Were Used: CORP\admin.ops
Target Server Name: FILE01
Process Name: C:\Windows\System32\runas.exe
Network Address: 10.x.x.x

queries.title: Retrieve Event ID 4648
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4648
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Microsoft Sentinel Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Apply the event-specific field and correlation guidance on this page after retrieving the base events.

queries.title: Retrieve Event ID 4648
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4648
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and field aliases vary by Splunk deployment and Windows add-on version.
Map provider fields from the raw event before implementing event-specific filtering or aggregation.

queries.title: Retrieve Event ID 4648
assumptions: The relevant Windows event channel is ingested through a Cortex XDR Windows event collection path.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed field names vary by collection path and event provider.

queries.title: Windows Event ID 4648
queries.query:
title: Windows Event ID 4648
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4648
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the field conditions and correlations documented on this page in the destination SIEM.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4648 mean?
faqs.answer: Event ID 4648 records an attempt to use explicitly supplied credentials, commonly through RUNAS or a process that calls LogonUser with explicit account data.

faqs.question: Does 4648 mean the supplied credentials succeeded?
faqs.answer: No. It records the explicit-credential attempt; correlate with later authentication and logon events on the target system to establish success.

faqs.question: Why is Event ID 4648 useful for lateral-movement analysis?
faqs.answer: It identifies the originating subject, the account whose credentials were supplied, the process, and the target server, allowing analysts to spot credential switching and unexpected remote targets.

sources.title: 4648: Microsoft Windows security auditing event reference
